_min_coverage_for_full_smart_money: 5 primary + 3 secondary = full §11. <5+3 = partial mode. 0+0 = §11 skipped.

primary.name: L Catterton
primary.framework_descriptor: Backs Vuori, Honest Company, Peloton, Tarte. Active thesis on premium athleisure share-shift to specialist brands. Brand advantage durable but requires reinvention every 5-7 years.
primary.public_communication_note: Direct competitor exposure (Vuori) · sector reports public

primary.name: Forerunner Ventures
primary.framework_descriptor: Glossier, Bonobos, Warby Parker, Hims early-stage. Thesis: brand-as-distribution structurally won by specialists, not legacy incumbents.

primary.name: Lerer Hippeau
primary.framework_descriptor: Allbirds, Casper, Warby Parker. View: brand fatigue real but slower than market prices in.

primary.name: Verlinvest
primary.framework_descriptor: Vita Coco, Hims, Tony's Chocolonely, Oatly. Hybrid public + private. Believes premium pricing power = compounding moat when category healthy.
primary.public_communication_note: Sector white papers · quarterly thesis posts

primary.name: Maveron
primary.framework_descriptor: Allbirds, Trupanion, eBay (early). Howard Schultz-affiliated. Lens: consumer trust + repeat purchase > one-time hype.

secondary.name: Pershing Square
secondary.framework_descriptor: Bill Ackman. Quality consumer at distress: Chipotle, Restaurant Brands, Howard Hughes. Framework: simple model + cash-generative + activist lever + brand moat.

secondary.name: Akre Capital
secondary.framework_descriptor: Long-term holds in Mastercard, Constellation Software, Moody's. 'Three-legged stool' framework: business + management + reinvestment runway.

secondary.name: Polen Capital
secondary.framework_descriptor: Concentrated quality growth book. Has historically held LULU. Framework: high ROIC + brand moat + secular tailwind.

primary.name: Bessemer Venture Partners
primary.framework_descriptor: Cloud Index publisher, State of the Cloud reports, deep public-comp visibility. Frameworks on NDR, magic number, rule of 40.

primary.name: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z)
primary.framework_descriptor: Marc Andreessen / partners. Thesis-driven essays on infrastructure, AI, dev tools.

primary.name: Index Ventures
primary.framework_descriptor: Stripe, Slack, Datadog, Confluent early. Frameworks on bottom-up adoption, developer tools.

primary.name: ICONIQ Growth
primary.framework_descriptor: Growth-stage SaaS / enterprise. Publishes benchmark reports.
primary.public_communication_note: Quarterly benchmark reports on SaaS metrics

primary.name: Battery Ventures
primary.framework_descriptor: Vertical SaaS, infrastructure, dev tools. Multi-stage approach.

secondary.name: Coatue Management
secondary.framework_descriptor: Public + private tech. Quarterly market reports. Thesis-led, growth-quality balance.

secondary.name: Lone Pine Capital
secondary.framework_descriptor: Steve Mandel / successors. Quality growth focus. Holdings include Microsoft, Meta, Amazon at various points.
secondary.public_communication_note: Limited public commentary; positions visible via 13-F

secondary.name: Stratechery (Ben Thompson)
secondary.framework_descriptor: Aggregation theory, platform economics. Most-read public tech analyst in the industry.
secondary.public_communication_note: Daily updates; publicly indispensable for tech-PM thinking

primary.name: Khosla Ventures
primary.framework_descriptor: Deep tech / chips / climate. Vinod Khosla.

primary.name: Lux Capital
primary.framework_descriptor: Frontier tech, semis, robotics, defense.

primary.name: Founders Fund
primary.framework_descriptor: Peter Thiel-affiliated. Hardware (Anduril, Palantir).

secondary.name: SemiAnalysis (Dylan Patel)
secondary.framework_descriptor: Independent semis analyst. Datacenter, GPU, AI infra deep technical.

secondary.name: Fabricated Knowledge
secondary.framework_descriptor: Doug O'Laughlin. Semis, hardware capex cycles.

Tech/Hardware._note: Hardware sector has fewer 5+3 (currently 3+2). Skill should accept partial registries — render §11 with however many firms are present, note coverage gap in §14 Data Gaps.

primary.name: RA Capital
primary.framework_descriptor: Biotech specialist crossover.

primary.name: OrbiMed
primary.framework_descriptor: Healthcare-only. Public + private.

primary.name: Bessemer Healthcare
primary.framework_descriptor: Bessemer's healthcare arm. Healthcare Atlas reports.

secondary.name: Endpoints News
secondary.framework_descriptor: Industry trade publication with strong analytical voice.

Healthcare/Pharma._note: Partial coverage (3+1). Add more firms as encountered.

primary.name: Ribbit Capital
primary.framework_descriptor: Fintech specialist. Robinhood, Nubank, Coinbase early.

primary.name: QED Investors
primary.framework_descriptor: Fintech-only. Frank Rotman.

Fintech._note: Thin coverage (2+1). Add more firms as encountered.

secondary.name: Goehring & Rozencwajg
secondary.framework_descriptor: Natural resources / energy specialists. High-quality public commentary.

secondary.name: Andurand Capital
secondary.framework_descriptor: Pierre Andurand. Public + private commentary on oil cycles.

Energy._note: Energy has very few public-facing VCs. Currently 0 primary + 2 secondary. §11 will run in heavily-degraded mode for energy stocks until expanded.

Healthcare/Devices._note: Empty — populate as encountered. §11 will skip for tickers in this sector.

primary.name: Andreessen Horowitz Games
primary.framework_descriptor: a16z Games vertical led by Andrew Chen, Jonathan Lai, Marc Andreessen. Thesis: network effects + creator economy + UGC moat are the durable platform advantages; AI-native platforms are the next wave; multiplayer-real-time differs structurally from video-latent-space generation. Active investments: Sky Mavis (Axie), Discord, Adobe (creator tools), Mythical, Roblox-adjacent infra. Wrote canonical 'Why every company will be a games company' essays.
primary.public_communication_note: a16z.com/games + Andrew Chen Substack + a16z podcast

primary.name: Bessemer Venture Partners
primary.framework_descriptor: Cloud Index publisher + State of the Cloud reports + Atlas platform analytics. Frameworks for platform companies: Rule of 40 (growth + FCF margin), magic number, NDR, gross margin trajectory. The platform-equivalent metrics for Communications names focus on engagement-monetization gap, deferred-revenue mechanics, and FCF inflection points.
primary.public_communication_note: Atlas reports quarterly; State of the Cloud annual

primary.name: Sequoia Capital
primary.tier_descriptor: Generalist with deep platform + creator economy bench
primary.framework_descriptor: Long history of consumer + platform investments: Discord, Reddit, Faze Clan, YouTube (early), Square, Stripe. Framework: founder-market fit + platform compounding + 'long-term greedy'. Sequoia partner essays emphasize that platform success compounds over decades, not quarters; market sentiment volatility on platform names is opportunity for long-horizon holders.

primary.name: Index Ventures
primary.framework_descriptor: Stripe, Slack, Datadog, Confluent, Figma early. Platform thesis: bottom-up adoption + developer relations + extensibility moat. Applied to Communications/UGC names: creator economy + developer toolset + monetization economy combined produce durable moats unavailable to closed-ecosystem platforms.

primary.name: Insight Partners
primary.framework_descriptor: ScaleUp playbook for growth-stage platforms. Framework: durable software economics scale through 'GTM efficiency × retention × pricing power'. Applied to Communications platforms: focus on net-revenue retention by cohort, payment platform mix (direct vs app store), and creator economy DevEx as leading indicators of long-term margin trajectory.
primary.public_communication_note: Views blog + ScaleUp Society community insights

secondary.name: Stratechery (Ben Thompson)
secondary.framework_descriptor: Aggregation Theory: aggregators win by owning user demand; platforms win by owning supply. Has written specifically about Roblox (UGC platform thesis), Meta (cross-feed aggregation), Spotify (commoditizing music label suppliers), Netflix (content as moat vs aggregation). Daily Updates + Weekly Article. The most influential public framework for analyzing Communications-sector business models.
secondary.public_communication_note: Weekly Article free; Daily Update subscription. Search via stratechery.com/?s={ticker}

secondary.name: Matthew Ball
secondary.tier_descriptor: Independent metaverse analyst, author of 'The Metaverse'
secondary.framework_descriptor: Wrote the canonical multi-part Roblox primer (2020-2022) and the 2022 book 'The Metaverse and How It Will Revolutionize Everything'. Framework: 'The 7 rules of the Metaverse' — interoperability, persistence, real-time, network effects, content creators, payment systems, and identity. Publishes regular research on gaming, streaming, and platform economics.
secondary.public_communication_note: matthewball.co essays free; quarterly research notes

secondary.name: Coatue Management
secondary.tier_descriptor: Public + private tech crossover hedge fund
secondary.framework_descriptor: Quality growth at scale, secular AI / platform thesis. Has historically held RBLX, META, GOOGL, NFLX. Framework: tech-quality balance — pair growth with operating margin trajectory + FCF compounding. Quarterly market reports cover platform names with multi-year horizon.
